'''Douglas III''', the third [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the remote [[Douglas system/Legends|Douglas system]], was a member of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Far from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Galactic Core]] and of little interest to the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Douglas III was made famous by the game [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mendel Douglas]], who failed to return from the planet during a quest to acquire the hide of Douglas III's elusive native [[jackelope]]. The planet later attracted [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Black market/Legends|black-marketeers]].

==Description==
Located far from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Galactic Core]],<ref name="SWR" /> Douglas III,<ref name="Gamer" /> also referred to simply as Douglas,<ref name="Atlas" /> was the third [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the remote [[Douglas system/Legends|Douglas system]].<ref name="Gamer" /> It was positioned at the terminus of the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] region and [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Covered by landmasses and expanses of [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]], Douglas III was of very little interest to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large.<ref name="SWR" /> Nevertheless, as the home of the [[jackelope]],<ref name="CSWE-Douglas III" /> a small horned creature,<ref name="CSWE-Jackelope">{{CSWECite|II|132|jackelope}}</ref> Douglas III became one of several Centrality planets notorious for its hostile wildlife.<ref name="Atlas" />

==History==
The Douglas [[Star system/Legends|system]] owed its name to the game [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mendel Douglas]],<ref name="Gamer" /> who made Douglas III famous<ref name="CSWE-Douglas III" /> after traversing its surface in search of the elusive<ref name="Gamer" /> and rare<ref name="CSWE-Douglas III" /> jackelope, hoping to collect the creature's hide. While the jackelope ultimately made it back for his collection, Douglas himself did not.<ref name="Gamer" />

Circa [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'' is the first installment, between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> the working-class [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] [[Poly Pyramid]] on the planet [[Rafa IV]] adorned its interior with incompetently [[Taxidermist|taxidermized]] [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] fauna either nailed to the walls or hanging from the ceiling. Among the decorative creatures was a jackelope from Douglas III.<ref name="Mindharp of Sharu">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref> By the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Douglas III had become home to [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Black market/Legends|black-market]] [[Merchant/Legends|traders]].<ref name="SWR" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TheMindharpofSharu.png|thumb|right|300px|Douglas III was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu''.]]
Douglas III was first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]'', the [[1983]] first entry in the [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels written by [[L. Neil Smith]].<ref name="Mindharp of Sharu" /> According to [[Star Wars Insider 145|''Star Wars Insider'' 145]]<ref name="Insider 145">{{InsiderCite|145|Authors of the Expanded Universe|Authors of the Expanded Universe: L. Neil Smith}}</ref>—published by [[October 21]], [[2013]]<ref name="Insider145 date">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-insider-145-from-topps-to-gold-bikini-bottoms|text=''Star Wars Insider'' #145: From Topps to (Gold Bikini) Bottoms|nolive=1}}</ref>—Smith [[Tuckerization|cannibalized]] the names of colleagues to create names for Douglas III and [[Paulking XIV|other]] [[Arleen|subjects]] from the book.<ref name="Insider 145" /> In popular culture, twentieth-century [[Wikipedia:Taxidermy|taxidermist]] Douglas Herrick is credited with creating the American [[Wikipedia:Jackalope|jackalope]]—a legendary creature with a [[Wikipedia:Hare|jackrabbit's]] body and the antlers of a [[Wikipedia:Pronghorn|pronghorn antelope]]—which developed into a tourist attraction in Herrick's hometown of [[Wikipedia:Douglas, Wyoming|Douglas, Wyoming]].<ref name="NY Times">{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2003/01/19/us/douglas-herrick-82-dies-father-of-west-s-jackalope.html|text=Douglas Herrick, 82, Dies; Father of West's Jackalope|author=Martin, Douglas|date=2003-01-19|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20180907130246}}</ref>

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed Douglas III in the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it is considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> Douglas III was later one of 200 playable worlds included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which provided the planet's only visual depiction, an image also used to illustrate eighteen other planets of similar composition in the game. ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' and its accompanying [[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] also placed Douglas III in the Jospro [[Sector/Legends|sector]],<ref name="SWR" /><ref name="SWR Prima">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> but author [[Michael Kogge]]'s [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]" from [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]],<ref name="Gamer" /> which was published on [[July 24]], [[2001]],<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> later changed the planet's location to the Centrality,<ref name="Gamer" /> a placement eventually confirmed by the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which placed the planet, referred to by the shortened name "Douglas," in grid square U-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="Atlas" />
